title: Art Directors
description: Formulate design concepts and presentation approaches for visual productions and media, such as print, broadcasting, video, and film. Direct workers engaged in artwork or layout design.

tasks.text: Work with creative directors to develop design solutions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Present final layouts to clients for approval.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manage own accounts and projects, working within budget and scheduling requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with creative, art, copywriting, or production department heads to discuss client requirements and presentation concepts and to coordinate creative activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with clients to determine objectives, budget, background information, and presentation approaches, styles, and techniques.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Formulate basic layout design or presentation approach and specify material details, such as style and size of type, photographs, graphics, animation, video, and sound.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review and approve art materials, copy materials, and proofs of printed copy developed by staff members.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create custom illustrations or other graphic elements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attend photo shoots and printing sessions to ensure that the products needed are obtained.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review illustrative material to determine if it conforms to standards and specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Hire, train, and direct staff members who develop design concepts into art layouts or who prepare layouts for printing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research current trends and new technology, such as printing production techniques, computer software, and design trends.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mark up, paste, and complete layouts and write typography instructions to prepare materials for typesetting or printing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conceptualize and help design interfaces for multimedia games, products, and devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare detailed storyboards showing sequence and timing of story development for television production.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Negotiate with printers and estimators to determine what services will be performed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
